I reviewed all 10 supplied papers against the schema. Here is the structured assessment.

```json
  "tf": "KIF22",
  "query": "KIF22 TCR inhibition",
  "papers_retrieved": 10,

  "paper_assessments": [
    {

      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "KIF22 promotes MM progression by regulating the CDC25C/CDK1/cyclinB1 pathway.",
      "rationale": "KIF22 promotes myeloma cell-cycle progression; not T cells and no TCR signalling readout."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC9398805",
      "retrieval_rank": 4,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; non-immune (thyroid cancer tissues and TPC-1/KTC-1 cells, mouse)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "KIF11 knockdown remarkably suppressed thyroid cancer cell proliferation as well as induced apoptosis.",
      "rationale": "Concerns KIF11, a different gene, in thyroid cancer; not KIF22 and not TCR."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11237943",
      "retrieval_rank": 5,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; T cells (TCR signalling in human T cells)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Ser325 phosphorylation promotes PTPN22's TCR-inhibitory activity in human T cells.",
      "rationale": "Addresses PTPN22-mediated TCR inhibition, a different regulator; provides no evidence about KIF22."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC10824666",
      "retrieval_rank": 6,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; non-immune (ovarian/breast cancer cell lines, mouse models)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Inhibitors of the KIF18A motor protein selectively kill chromosomally unstable cancer cells.",
      "rationale": "Concerns KIF18A, a different gene, in cancer mitosis; not KIF22 and not TCR."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12346228",
      "retrieval_rank": 7,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "review",
      "experimental_system": "Human; T cells (TCR repertoire for adoptive therapy, review)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Explores using patient TCR repertoire data to select T cells for adoptive immunotherapy.",
      "rationale": "Review of therapeutic TCR selection; no mention of KIF22 or TCR inhibition mechanism."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC10907621",
      "retrieval_rank": 8,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; non-immune (cancer cell lines)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "Weak basal APC/C activity makes cancer cells uniquely sensitive to KIF18A inhibition.",
      "rationale": "Concerns KIF18A, a different gene, in cancer mitosis; not KIF22 and not TCR."

    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11014665",

      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; engineered CD4 TCR-T cells, mouse xenograft nasopharyngeal tumor model",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "CD4 TCR-T cells targeting EBNA1 significantly inhibited nasopharyngeal tumor growth in mouse models.",
      "rationale": "TCR-engineered therapy inhibits tumor growth; no KIF22 and no TCR-inhibition phenotype."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC6996685",
      "retrieval_rank": 10,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Human; non-immune (tongue squamous carcinoma tissues/CAL-27, SCC-15 cells, mouse xenograft)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "KIF22 suppression inhibited cell proliferation and xenograft tumor growth in tongue carcinoma.",
      "rationale": "KIF22 drives carcinoma proliferation; no T cell or TCR inhibition context."

  ],
  "overall_evidence_tier": "none",
  "key_paperclip_result_ids": [],
  "tier_rationale": "No retrieved paper materially links KIF22 to TCR inhibition or a closely related inhibitory T cell phenotype. KIF22 papers (PMC11233920, PMC7176455, PMC11076398, PMC6996685) address mitosis/proliferation in non-T-cell cancer or chondrocyte systems. TCR-inhibition-relevant papers concern other genes/topics: PMC11237943 is PTPN22, PMC12346228 is a TCR-repertoire review, and PMC11014665 is TCR-T therapy. PMC9398805, PMC10824666 and PMC10907621 concern KIF11/KIF18A. None provide primary causal evidence connecting KIF22 to TCR signalling inhibition, checkpoint response, anergy, or exhaustion with impaired TCR function.",
  "confidence": "high"
}
```

**Summary:** None of the 10 retrieved papers provide evidence linking KIF22 to TCR inhibition. The four genuine KIF22 studies concern proliferation/mitosis in cancer and chondrocyte (non-T-cell) systems, while the papers touching TCR inhibition or T cell biology involve different genes (PTPN22, KIF11, KIF18A) or unrelated topics. Overall tier: **none** (high confidence).

parsed.tier_rationale: No retrieved paper materially links KIF22 to TCR inhibition or a closely related inhibitory T cell phenotype. KIF22 papers (PMC11233920, PMC7176455, PMC11076398, PMC6996685) address mitosis/proliferation in non-T-cell cancer or chondrocyte systems. TCR-inhibition-relevant papers concern other genes/topics: PMC11237943 is PTPN22, PMC12346228 is a TCR-repertoire review, and PMC11014665 is TCR-T therapy. PMC9398805, PMC10824666 and PMC10907621 concern KIF11/KIF18A. None provide primary causal evidence connecting KIF22 to TCR signalling inhibition, checkpoint response, anergy, or exhaustion with impaired TCR function.
